response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and systems software engineer with over 15 years of experience, but his background is almost entirely in web, mobile, and application-layer development. The Principal Performance Architect role demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, AI accelerator architecture, and GPU workload optimization — none of which appear anywhere in this resume. His closest relevant experience is writing low-latency async I/O applications at Dunnhumby and some C/C++ work at National Instruments, but these are distant from the silicon-level performance analysis and deep-learning workload optimization the role requires.
strengths: 15+ years of software engineering across multiple paradigms and languages demonstrates strong technical adaptability
Low-latency systems experience at Dunnhumby (async I/O, high-performance databases, millions of requests/sec) shows some performance-oriented thinking
C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and a wave field synthesis thesis project indicate at least baseline familiarity with lower-level computing
concerns: Zero SoC, IP modeling, or hardware architecture experience — the core technical requirement of the role
No AI/ML, deep learning, or neural network optimization background of any kind
No GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or hardware-software co-design experience; the entire career is application and web-layer focused

key_factors.factor: No AI/DL workload or accelerator expertise
